stream_a.title: EU vote on CBAM carbon price deduction rules delayed
stream_a.summary: EU member states did not vote last week on rules defining which foreign CO2 prices importers can deduct from their Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) fees, even though a decision had been scheduled.

stream_a.title: UK hospitals, small emitters cut emissions beyond ETS targets in 2025
stream_a.summary: Britain's hospitals and small industrial emitters cut their greenhouse gas emissions well below government targets in 2025, according to newly published data covering participants in the UK Emissions Trading Scheme's Hospital and Small Emitter (HSE) scheme.

stream_a.title: Euro Markets: EUAs hold on to €80 before ETS reform reports trigger heavy selling
stream_a.summary: European carbon managed to hold above the €80 mark for the first half of the day until news reports detailing a key political party's position on the EU ETS review triggered a heavy sell-off as traders reacted to perceived bearish supply-side proposals.

stream_a.title: Top EU Parliament group seeks free permit handouts for power providers, speculation limits in upcoming ETS reform -media
stream_a.summary: The European Parliament's largest political group is considering a push to grant power generators free EU Emissions Trading System (EU ETS) allowances after 2030 in exceptional circumstances, as well as limits to the participation of speculators in the market, according to a draft negotiating position seen by news outlet Montel.

stream_a.title: Europe’s industrial wake-up call
stream_a.summary: Europe’s climate ambitions must integrate with industrial competitiveness. The upcoming EU ETS reform should support decarbonization while ensuring affordable energy, investment certainty, energy security and a level playing field.

stream_a.title: Vietnam carbon price opens at $5.20/t in first day of trading
stream_a.summary: Vietnam's newly launched domestic carbon exchange opened trading on Monday with carbon allowances priced at VND 136,000 ($5.20) per tonne of CO2e, marking the pilot’s first official transaction as the Southeast Asian prepares to roll out an emissions trading scheme.

stream_b.title: Australian aviation company raises A$28 mln to scale drag-reducing tech
stream_b.summary: An Australian venture capital fund announced Tuesday it had successfully raised A$28 mln ($19.2 mln) on behalf of an aerospace company looking to scale its drag-reducing adhesive film that improves flight efficiency.

stream_b.title: 如何應對連日40度？一圖看懂高溫兵推、熱傷害急救
stream_b.summary: 近期歐洲多國面臨破紀錄高溫熱浪，甚至危及生命安全。歐洲高溫災難成為台灣借鏡，環境部啟動首場以北台灣連3天攝氏40度為基準的跨部會「高溫兵棋推演」，7月後將擇一縣市實兵演練。

stream_b.title: Canadian developer issued North America’s first certified DAC credits under long-term offtake deals
stream_b.summary: A Montreal-headquartered developer has been issued North America's first certified direct air capture (DAC) carbon removal (CDR) credits, setting up the first delivery under long-term purchase agreements extending through 2034.

stream_b.title: Experts draft global ocean carbon removals framework due in 2027
stream_b.summary: International marine science experts are drawing up a global framework to assess ocean-based climate interventions, such as marine carbon removal (mCDR), with the work due to be completed by mid-2027.

stream_b.title: Seaweed carbon removal faces major ocean trade-offs, study warns
stream_b.summary: Growing seaweed across vast areas of the ocean could help draw down more CO2, but the carbon removal gains would be limited compared with the amount of biomass produced and could come with major risks for marine ecosystems, a peer-reviewed study has found.

stream_b.title: New method could strengthen biochar carbon stability checks, study finds
stream_b.summary: A microscopy-based method could help assess whether biochar material has been sufficiently carbonised for long-term storage, a new study has found.

stream_b.title: European consortium lays groundwork for Mediterranean seagrass nature credits
stream_b.summary: A group of organisations presented on Monday a framework for Mediterranean seagrass restoration aimed at helping quantify ecosystem value and support the development of nature credits.

stream_b.title: FEATURE: Gross or net? EU wrestles with CO2 accounting rules for ETS removals
stream_b.summary: As the European Commission prepares legislation to bring carbon removals into the EU Emissions Trading System (ETS), experts warn that choices over CO2 accounting risk giving policymakers a serious headache in Brussels.

stream_b.summary: 友邦帛琉共和國農漁暨環境部長維史提訪台，維史提於外交部午宴感謝台灣協助帛琉舉辦「太平洋島國論壇」，歡迎台灣方參與相關會議；維史提此行將出席國合會30周年相關慶祝活動，另包括馬紹爾群島、瓜地馬拉、史瓦...

stream_b.title: INTERVIEW: Govts urged to use health budgets for urban nature-based solutions amid deadly European heatwave
stream_b.summary: Governments should allocate part of their health budgets to greening urban areas, as scaling up nature-based solutions is one of the most effective ways to tackle rising temperatures in European cities, an expert told Carbon Pulse.

stream_b.title: Most eligible CDM projects on course to miss out on Article 6 transition as deadline looms
stream_b.summary: The vast majority of carbon projects developed under the Kyoto Protocol's Clean Development Mechanism (CDM) are set to miss a Tuesday deadline to enable them to transition to UN crediting under the Paris Agreement, leaving many legacy projects outside the new scheme.

stream_b.title: 4友邦官員訪台慶國合會30週年 帛琉部長感謝台灣幫助
stream_b.summary: （中央社記者楊堯茹台北29日電）友邦帛琉共和國農漁暨環境部長維史提訪台，維史提於外交部午宴感謝台灣協助帛琉舉辦「太平洋島國論壇」，歡迎台灣方參與相關會議；維史提此行將出席國合會30週年相關慶祝活動，另包括馬紹爾群島、瓜地馬拉、史瓦帝尼等友邦部長也來台共襄盛舉。

stream_b.title: Singapore Airlines sees climate compliance costs as high as $155 mln
stream_b.summary: Singapore Airlines said climate‑related compliance costs were financially material, with exposure projected at between SGD 50-200 million ($38-155 mln), and high risk impacts above that.

stream_b.title: Brazilian lender backs new Amazon REDD+ project targeting 7.5 mln credits annually by 2030
stream_b.summary: A Brazilian state-controlled lender will support a newly launched Amazon REDD+ carbon credit initiative targeting 5 million hectares under management, annual production of 7.5 mln credits, and around $50 mln in net yearly revenue by 2030.

stream_b.title: Chile authorises second standalone battery storage project under Swiss Article 6 deal
stream_b.summary: Chile and Switzerland have authorised a second standalone battery energy storage system (BESS) mitigation activity under Article 6 of the Paris Agreement, expanding the use of carbon finance to support grid-scale energy storage in the South American country.

stream_b.title: Tanzania rejects claims carbon market deals surrender natural resources
stream_b.summary: Tanzania has rejected opposition claims that international carbon trading agreements amount to the sale or transfer of control over the country's natural resources, insisting that its carbon market framework is designed to protect national sovereignty while generating new revenue.

stream_b.title: 【南科沙崙案】從環評大亂鬥到坐下來對話：沙崙「生態協作平台」的初登板與酸甜苦辣
Conservation should not over-rely on carbon markets, Singaporean NbS expert cautions
stream_b.summary: Conservation finance risks creating a "monoculture" by relying too heavily on carbon markets, a prominent Singaporean conservation scientist has warned.

stream_b.title: UN draft sets Article 6.4 first transfer, real-time reporting rules
stream_b.summary: The UNFCCC’s latest draft procedure for the Article 6.4 mechanism registry sets out requirements for real-time public reporting of registry activity, detailed first-transfer rules, and proposed account fees for the Paris Agreement Crediting Mechanism (PACM).

stream_b.title: Cookstove projects draw fresh criticism in India as investigation alleges poor adoption, limited awareness
stream_b.summary: An investigation into cookstove projects in India's Karnataka state has found that some rural households rarely used the stoves they received, were unaware they were participating in carbon credit schemes, and did not receive any share of the revenues.

stream_b.title: 國土綠蔭說明會嘉義首啟 環境部邀種樹
stream_b.summary: （中央社記者蔡智明嘉義市29日電）環境部首場「國土綠蔭氣候調適計畫說明會」今天在嘉義大學舉辦，部長彭啓明說，面對極端高溫氣候，要凝聚全民共識打造降溫、減災的國土韌性綠網，邀大家一起來種樹。

stream_b.title: Farm-level GHG accounting methods need urgent harmonisation efforts, study says
stream_b.summary: Farm‑level greenhouse gas accounting methods remain fragmented and require urgent harmonisation efforts, with estimates for the same farm varying by more than 1,000%, a study said.

stream_b.title: Indonesia, LEAF Coalition operator sign J-REDD+ MoU
stream_b.summary: Indonesia has signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with a US-based nonprofit to explore opportunities for reducing emissions from its forests, with a focus on jurisdictional REDD+ approaches.
